seoTitle: How to Use Kanban Boards in Jira Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to navigate a Jira Kanban board, move cards across statuses, set WIP limits, and use swimlanes to organize continuous work.
intro: Letting a backlog pile up unorganized makes it hard to spot where work is actually getting stuck. This guide covers using a Jira Kanban board day to day — moving cards, setting column limits, and organizing work with swimlanes and filters.

steps.heading: Open the Kanban board
steps.body: Navigate to your software space and click the Kanban board option in the left navigation sidebar.

steps.heading: Move cards across statuses
steps.body: Click, drag, and drop an issue card from one column to the next, such as from To Do into In Progress, to update its status.

steps.heading: Configure your columns
steps.body: Open Board Settings and use the Columns configuration panel to rename columns, add workflow stages, or adjust issue limits.

steps.heading: Set work-in-progress limits
steps.body: Set a maximum WIP value on a column to help spot pipeline bottlenecks before they slow down the whole board.

steps.heading: Group with swimlanes and filters
steps.body: Open the Swimlanes tab to sort tasks horizontally by assignee, and apply quick filters like 'Only My Issues' to keep the board scannable.

faq.question: How do I move a card on a Jira Kanban board?
faq.answer: Click, hold, and drag the card from its current column into the new column to update its status.

faq.question: What is a WIP limit on a Jira board?
faq.answer: A work-in-progress limit caps how many issues can sit in a column at once, helping surface bottlenecks before they slow the team down.

faq.question: How do I add swimlanes to my Kanban board?
faq.answer: Open Board Settings, go to the Swimlanes tab, and choose a grouping option like assignee to sort tasks horizontally.

faq.question: Can I filter a Kanban board to show only my issues?
faq.answer: Yes, most boards include a quick filter toggle for showing only issues assigned to you.
